# Kaola-Workflow Roadmap

This file mirrors active unfinished work. GitHub issues are the source of truth when available.

## Active Work

| Issue | Title | Status | Workflow Project | Next Step |

| #745 | bug(plan-validator): interior code-reviewer/security-reviewer walls still false-block on disjoint downstream writes — the surface-freshness rescue covers only adversarial-verifier, and their reopen remedy still dead-ends | queued — epic #757 WAVE D; closes with a DISPOSITION NOTE, no implementation (reopen-remedy half superseded by #761) | — | SHIPPED: interiorSurfaceFresh widened to code-reviewer/security-reviewer, with the producer slice RECOMPUTED from the frozen graph (not trusted from the journal) and a final certifier counting as a live whole-candidate wall only when its ledger row is actually complete. REMAINING: the reopen remedy. Auto-parking a "provably pristine" sink was REJECTED as unsafe, not deferred — the sink runs main-session-direct and its first irreversible step writes no in-band marker, so a pristine sink cannot be distinguished from one that already committed/pushed/merged. A safe remedy needs an explicit sink-progress marker. Cross-epoch freshness (seal attempt absent after journal rotation) also still falls back to whole-candidate. RESHAPED 2026-07-22 under epic #757: the reopen-remedy half is SUPERSEDED by #761 (local re-expansion; reopen becomes rollback-only) — do not implement it here. When #762 lands, re-check whether the shipped freshness widening is still load-bearing: declared+amended-surface staleness removes the whole-candidate binding at the root, and the cross-epoch residual dissolves with it. |
| #756 | bug(finalize/reopen): finalize done-gate surfacing a required file outside a completed writer's frozen write set wedges a PASSED-review serial run (reopen strands, amend orphans journal, replan needs a failed attempt) | queued — epic #757 WAVE D; acceptance HARNESS for #761 + #762, not an independent fix | — | RESHAPED 2026-07-22 under epic #757 — this issue is now the ACCEPTANCE HARNESS for #761 (local re-expansion: removes dead-ends 3/4/6, no failed-attempt prerequisite) + #762 (declared-not-walled surfaces: removes dead-ends 1/2/5); do NOT batch with the #745 reopen-remedy half (superseded by #761). Of the three candidate directions, direction 1 was chosen and generalized; direction 2 (tail-first cascade) is unnecessary (reopen stays rollback-only); direction 3 (completeness lint) is downgraded to an optional planner aid. A run whose review PASSED wedges at finalize when a project done-gate (not modeled as a DAG node) surfaces a required code-relevant file outside every frozen write set: barrier-check refuses write_set_overflow, verdict-check refuses verdict_not_pass (the code-relevant file stales the certifier candidate; docs correctly do not), reopen-node on the writer refuses would_strand_completed_dependent, on the gate refuses would_orphan_in_progress then would_strand, write-set amend + freeze --repair refuses review_journal_plan_hash_mismatch, and replan refuses replan_source_journal_missing because it requires a FAILED-review attempt a passed review never produces. Three candidate directions in the issue: (1) a sanctioned expand-and-re-review primitive that does not require a prior failed attempt, (2) reopen-node tail-first cascade instead of a hard would_strand refusal, (3) a planner companion-file completeness lint. Acceptance requires the recovery keep the new file REVIEWED, not merely attributed. WAVE C CARRY-FORWARD (from #762, landed 2026-07-23): #762 shipped the BARRIER half — an attributable out-of-surface companion file now appends an append-only exact-file `amend(<point>)` block, clears the whole-plan write_set_overflow barrier once its owning point is complete, and ROUTES to re-review via #761 reexpand-open (wall→pending, gate-verify obligation raised). What #762 explicitly did NOT do and handed here: COMPLETING the wall's in-plan re-review after re-open still hits `review_repair_delta_unavailable` (settled-pass attempt, no fold boundary to derive a repair delta from) — the same epoch-transition / repair-delta problem this harness must exercise. So the harness must prove the recovered file ends REVIEWED (not merely attributed): amend-surface → reexpand-open re-opens the owning milestone's writer+wall, and the wall re-discharge must produce a real review verdict, which needs the settled-pass fold boundary resolved. Acceptance = #756 dead-ends 1/2/5 gone (barrier, via #762) AND 3/4/6 gone (re-open, via #761) AND the wall actually re-reviews to PASS in-plan. MINOR RIDER (from #763 MPfused, non-blocking): the fused-advance (close-and-open-next) dispatch site's `context_packet` attach lacks a direct coverage test — shipped code verified correct by the #763 verifier, only the assertion is missing; fold a one-line dispatch-card assertion into this harness while it exercises the fused-advance path. |
| #757 | epic(adaptive): progressive elaboration — coarse frozen spine + just-in-time expansion, parallel-by-default | IN PROGRESS — Wave A DONE (#758/#729/#759 CLOSED); Wave B DONE 2026-07-23 (#761 local re-expansion, #760 serialization inversion, #763 context packet — ALL CLOSED; merges 6ef8752c, 348670c6, ab825a5f; four edition chains green on the merged tree). Wave C DONE 2026-07-23 (#762 declared-not-walled surfaces, #730-AC9 unified repair feedback — both CLOSED; merges d7aefce1/4c2a137a, edition-sync regenerated the forge ports, all four chains green on the synced tree). NOW: Wave D (#766 opencode/kimi port sweep ∥ #756 acceptance harness for #761+#762 ∥ #745 disposition close) — co-open, file-disjoint. REMAINING: Wave E (#765 cutover ALONE → close epic). | — | EXECUTION SCHEDULE — five waves, run STRICTLY IN ORDER. WAVE A (DONE): #759 expansion transaction (adaptive-node.js, next-action.js) ∥ #729 remaining ACs (replan.js). WAVE B (DONE 2026-07-23): #761 local re-expansion (adaptive-node.js, replan.js) ∥ #760 serialization inversion (adaptive-schema.js, routing skeletons+slots, adaptive-node.js) ∥ #763 context packet + efficiency line (adaptive-node.js) — merges 6ef8752c/348670c6/ab825a5f, four chains green. WAVE C (NOW): #762 declared-not-walled surfaces (plan-validator.js, adaptive-schema.js, adaptive-node.js) ∥ #730 AC-9 unify direct-repair and replan-child feedback delivery (replan.js) — file-disjoint, co-open; #762 also carries #761's M7b integration-coverage and #763's MPfused fused-advance-site carry-forwards. WAVE D: #766 opencode/kimi port sweep (additive trees only) ∥ #756 acceptance harness for #761+#762 (walkthrough) ∥ #745 disposition close (docs only). WAVE E: #765 cutover ALONE, then close this epic. CRITICAL PATH is #759 → #761 → #762 → #766 → #765 and every edge on it is a NAMED S1 data dependency, so the wave count cannot be compressed below five: #761 consumes the expansion-record shape from #759; #762 routes amendments to re-review THROUGH #761's re-expansion record; #766 ports the settled prose of all core children; #765 ends dual-accept only after a full run has completed on the spine shape. Everything off that path is scheduled at the earliest wave its own dependency allows — #760 and #763 need only #759, so they co-open with #761 in WAVE B rather than waiting on it. CO-OPEN EVIDENCE — #761/#760/#763 all write adaptive-node.js, but shared-file-ness is a conflict FORECAST, not a serializer: S1 requires naming a concrete artifact one unit consumes from another, and none of the three consumes anything from the other two. They co-open in isolated legs and reconcile at the join; merge order is #761 (largest, region-replacing) then #763 (smallest, additive) then #760, so each reconcile is against a settled base. #730 AC-9 is held to WAVE C on real S1 evidence, not caution: it rewrites the replan-child feedback path in replan.js, and #761 retires replan_source_journal_missing in that same file — the artifact is named, so it waits. CARRY-FORWARD RULE (binding for every wave) — a wave is not finished when its code merges; it is finished when everything it DISCOVERED has a named owner. Each producer must return descoped items explicitly, and each descoped item must be written into the roadmap source of the child that will own it BEFORE the producing wave merges. Never leave a discovery only in a workflow journal or an agent transcript: those are ephemeral and this epic has already generated four such items. If a discovery fits no existing child, file a GitHub issue and add its .roadmap source in the same action. A verifier finding that is argued non-blocking still counts as a discovery and still needs an owner — the #758 spine validation-policy escape was carried into #759 this way and closed there. WAVE A CARRY-FORWARD, already assigned: #759's four descopes went to #761 (expansion_id as a validated review-journal attempt field), #762 (composed gate units invisible to --gate-verify / --verdict-check, fails closed), #763 (workflow-tasks.json mirror omits composed units, cosmetic), and #765 (six workflow-init surfaces + resume/frontier-batch cards move as one write set with the cutover); #729's surviving mutants are hardened in Wave A itself before merge. Per-child planning deltas live as 2026-07-22 comments on each issue. Principle prose was propagated ahead of mechanics at commit ad196273 (axioms+embeds, planner md+tomls, plan-run skeleton ×12) with chains DEFERRED — that debt is DISCHARGED as of the #758 ship, which ran all four chains green. Migration is dual-accept (legacy full-DAG plans keep byte-identical behavior); the cutover decision is an ADR at epic close. Hard anchors that must NOT loosen: consent valve, guard prologue, atomic freeze, reviewed-before-sink wall, finalize receipts, running-set ledger atomicity, unattributed-write tamper refusal. STRATEGY (user directive 2026-07-22): evidence-backed serialization — serial requires present-tense nameable evidence for S1/S2/S3; predictions and uncertainty are inadmissible; mode is evidence-governed, width stays with faithful decomposition (see the #760 addendum). |

## Rules

- A separate roadmap/research session owns discovering and adding future work to GitHub issues.
- `workflow-next` fetches GitHub issues, mirrors active implementation work here, and advances one item per cycle.
- After each workflow cycle, refresh this file from issue state.
- Move completed workflow project folders to `kaola-workflow/archive/`.
- Close linked GitHub issues only after acceptance criteria pass.
